62505107

Kek a so called Everest maxi and you couldn’t even put together ERC-3643

62505281
ERC-3643: The Compliance Standard for RWAs
ERC-3643 (also known as T-REX) is an open-source token standard originally developed by Tokeny (now part of Apex Group). It embeds compliance directly into tokens — KYC/AML checks, transfer restrictions, investor eligibility, clawbacks, etc. It’s designed for regulated security tokens and RWAs, making it easier for institutions to issue and manage compliant assets on public blockchains. stellar.org
Stellar’s Official Integration into ERC-3643
In 2025, the Stellar Development Foundation (SDF) formally joined the ERC3643 Association. This is a major move to extend the ERC-3643 compliance framework beyond EVM chains (like Ethereum/Polygon) to Stellar (via Soroban smart contracts).
Key goals:
• Support issuers bringing ERC-3643-compliant assets onto Stellar.
• Enable cross-chain interoperability between Stellar and Ethereum-based standards.
• Accelerate institutional RWA adoption with unified compliance tooling. stellar.org
Apex Group / Tokeny (the creators of ERC-3643) are deeply embedded in this ecosystem. Apex is a massive global fund administrator (servicing trillions in assets) and has committed to T-REX Ledger (a compliance layer tied to ERC-3643) as its default multi-chain infrastructure, with big tokenized asset targets.

62505284
>>62505281
This creates a standards bridge: Institutions using Apex/Tokeny tools for compliant issuance can now more easily deploy on Stellar (fast, cheap, payments-focused) while maintaining the same regulatory controls.
How This Connects to DTCC, Everest, and Europe/MiCA
• DTCC on Stellar (2027): Tokenized securities (stocks, ETFs, Treasuries) coming to Stellar. These are institutional-grade RWAs that need strong compliance for global distribution.
• ERC-3643 + Stellar: Provides the standardized compliance layer (investor rules, permissions) that makes DTCC-style assets easier to issue and manage on Stellar while being interoperable with other chains.
• Europe/MiCA: Stellar itself doesn’t need a MiCA license, but services to EU users do. MiCA-licensed players (like Everest) become the regulated on-ramps/wallets/custody solutions. Everest’s SCF Kickstart win and native Stellar wallet integration position it perfectly to serve European users accessing these compliant RWAs.
• Apex/Tokeny Role: As a major player in ERC-3643, Apex can act as (or partner with) issuance/transfer agents. Their tools + Stellar = scalable, compliant RWA infrastructure. Everest can integrate or complement this for end-user EU access.

That’s enough to buy a house, whether or not you actually want one. 2) selling too soon, or just because you are up, or because you’re afraid it will fall. do the math for me.

like actually:
How much does each launch cost?
take the total cost of an orbital data center and divide it by the total PB of the center (would use EB, but the math would be too unweildy)
be sure to include the costs of the solar power panels and anything else.
Remember that you will need abotu 2sq meters of panel per PB, so 10,000 sq meters of solar panels. the cost of the panels is relatively reasonable. the cost of getting them into space undamaged and then assembling them does not seem as reasonable
Now figure out how much income that data center makes per PB (on earth, the rental rate per PB is about $2500/month, but that includes $1000+ in power usage) and then you discount for profit for the renter.

so lets say it is a 5 EB orbital center, so a little over 6000 PB makes maybe $4.2 million/month

each launch costs abotu $75 million, so it would take 18 months of profiit to pay for one launch. I estimate it would take 18 months to pay for each launch.

Assume they get 50% more efficient at scale, that is still 9 months of operations per launch to equal one terrestrial data center.

Now factor in india launching at 10% the cost. How will space x make any money?


Now do the cost of repairs and upgrades. A benefit of a terrestrial datacenter is that you just send bob and achmed over in a truck to upgrade. Now do that in space.

I do this 3-4 times while fucking around on 4chan, then I edit it myself and change a lot of the formatting. Basically takes a day to do what used to take me 3 days.

>you then have to provide an actual service to gain users and profit.
So how do you profit from being an XRPL node?

62509565
>>62509520
You don't.
The people that run them are interested in a system that works properly that they can then build their own actual service on top of.
There is no benefit to running a node. You don't make money running a node or running tx for anyone.

The benefit to you comes after, ONLY if you provide a service on top of the infrastructure that is worth using, and that you can then charge for THAT SERVICE.
Incentivizing running the node makes it fake and gay.
Not incentivizing running nodes mean that people have to UP FRONT contribute value to regular people to the degree that trust is built and they'll eventually use your services for other activities.
This also leads to you and me, being able to use it for free.

It's about the middle man part. The saying is tongue in cheek.

I have a very good business. ALL THE TIME, I send people elsewhere for a better price or selection. This long term means I'm a trusted member of the community and I profit eventually, in a non scam manner.
But I have to provide actual value FIRST. And the value isn't making sure I get every sale. My value is being a highly trusted business person.

The type of incentive rules the type of operation you'll have. I'm a good person, so my incentive is just that.
But there are bad people, so you build a system that basically keeps them at bay or forces them to actually do something of value to get value themselves.
No incentive, is the best incentive, because it keeps total scum out of the operation.
I'll be taking lunch now at my whim.

62509571
>>62509565
>You don't.
You said "you have to provide an actual service to profit".
Now you're saying you don't profit.

Which is it?

62510068
>>62509571
They maintain the ledger FOR FREE.
This allows them to create a profitable product on top.
I don't have to use their service. I get to use the XRPL for free.
Meanwhile every other chain charges you and grifts just to do a tx. So their incentive is what?
High tx fees, unnecessary tx, etc.
When you don't get paid, and will only get paid if the system works very well and you provide a service ON TOP OF the base layer functionality, then you can make money.

I'm only going on for others that lurk. Please stop pretending to not understand though. It's not good for your psyche.

62510081
>>62510068
>They maintain the ledger FOR FREE.
>This allows them to create a profitable product on top.
so why is no incentive a good thing for maintaining the ledger, but not a good thing for the product on top?

62510090
>>62508427
That’s like saying S&P doesn’t hold any USD you stupid retarded faggot

62510097
>>62509401
Odd way of saying antisemetic

62510163
>>62510081
Because I don't have to pay for the product on top? Because then the chain and the base function is free of grift?

The burn of XRP for each tx, which disappears forever, insures that the network isn't spammed.
You don't need someone making money to stop the spam and control volume.

The second someone makes money on the tx is the second grifters create entire companies who's sole purpose is to funnel tx to the processing grifters.
We all know it costs the same to send money as it does an email. It's, for all intents and purposes free and the actual costs can't even be measured using thousandths of pennies. It's that close to free.

I'm glad you are asking real questions now.

62510196
>>62510163
>The second someone makes money on something is the second grifters create entire companies who's sole purpose is to funnel that something to the processing grifters.
So according to you, all products built on top of the XRPL are grifts by definition.

62510319
>>62510196
I'm not sure that logically follows.
You can build a grift on the XRPL if you like.
But it won't effect my ability to use the ledger for free so I'll probably not participate.

This is why there is no "meme coin" or "shit coin casino" on the XRPL.
The people that push and make the money on those are the people processing tx. They encourage it because the people running the nodes clean house and make a ton of money.
So they are promoted and exist THERE, not on the XRPL.
They have been tried, but you never get any traction because you don't have the middle man grifters getting paid no matter what.

You get more grift operations when they are promoted by very large grifters.
Like my business, I can run a grift here. I can put up SALE SALE SALE, 80% OFF!! and try to get people through the door.
But when they find out it's not and it's still a ripoff it won't work.

HOWEVER, if I got money every time someone pulled in the lot...I'd probably just do that all the time and not care that my sale was fake.
Why do I care you find out it's fake and gay after I got paid because you pulled in the lot?

62510355
>>62509200
>No one even makes money on the tx of XRP.
Be that as it may, Ripple Labs is raking in almost $1B a year from the XRP ecosystem (excluding token sales). NOTHING of that money flows back into said ecosystem.

https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/crypto/articles/ripple-xrp-ceo-targets-1b-151834617.html

No incentive (for you) is the best incentive (for us, Ripple Labs) LMAO

62510438
>>62510355
Yea, they make money by selling XRP. They created it. Makes sense.
Now...what is their incentive here? To sell it right?
What are people buying and why?

Their incentive is to make it worth buying, which means administering an efficient chain with no fees so that once you by, you can use it as you please.
Their incentive, especially given their holdings are in mid term (years) escrow, is to build something people want to buy into.
If it weren't for Ripple, I'd not own XRP actually.
They benefit THE MOST from building something worth using.
Because, no one has ANY REASON AT ALL to buy otherwise.

If they got mining and tx fees, then their incentive to make the most money would depend on what?
Pumping volume and scams and getting as many tx as possible.
So, you see...incentive. They make the most money when the tool is the most useful. Not when it's just used.
Places like Tranglo don't buy a billion of them otherwise.

If they made money on tx, you'd see a massive shitcoin casino.
But they don't. They make money on it being a legitimately useful product. Thusly, no shitcoin casino at all.

62510521
>>62510355
Except for spending billions buying banking and finance companies to have a vertically integrated complete financial system owned by ripple

62510526
>>62508440
You predicted it anon

62510565
Just as I predicted: the only refutes of ripple and xrp are logical fallacies/ridicule/ad hominems
All the btc treasuries imploding really has them ass blasted

62510719
>>62510319
>I'm not sure that logically follows.
It do.
You're saying as soon as someone makes money off something, grifters take hold.

Therefore all products built on top of XRP are grifts, since that's where the money and profit is made according to you.

Because making XRPL incentiveless just moves the incentive to the products built on top. It doesn't remove anything.
